title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You have read an article in an international magazine arguing that individual actions to protect the environment are pointless unless governments take large-scale action. Write a letter to the magazine editor responding to this argument and expressing your own view.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Editor,

I am writing in response to the article published in your last issue, which claimed that individual efforts to protect the environment are essentially meaningless without government intervention. While I agree that large-scale policy is crucial, I believe this argument dangerously underestimates the power of individual action.

Every significant social change in history has begun with individuals making different choices. The decisions we make daily — what we eat, how we travel, what we buy — collectively shape markets and send signals to governments and corporations alike. When millions of people reduce their meat consumption or choose public transport, industries are forced to adapt.

Furthermore, dismissing personal responsibility can lead to a dangerous sense of helplessness. If individuals believe their actions make no difference, apathy will follow — and that benefits nobody.

Of course, government policy is essential. But the two are not in conflict. Individual action and systemic change must work together. Both are necessary, and neither alone is sufficient.

Yours faithfully,
Nadia Kovács

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Riley has just moved into their first flat and wants to live more sustainably. Write an email to Riley offering practical advice on how to reduce their environmental impact at home, and sharing what you do in your own life to be more eco-friendly.

Write your email to Riley.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Riley,

Congratulations on the new flat! How exciting! And good for you for wanting to make it an eco-friendly home from the start — it's much easier to build good habits when you're setting things up fresh.

Here are a few tips that have really worked for me. The simplest change is switching to reusable bags, bottles, and containers — it sounds obvious but it makes a huge difference over time. Try to buy loose fruit and vegetables rather than pre-packaged ones, and look for supermarkets that offer refill stations for cleaning products.

In terms of energy, I'd recommend switching all your light bulbs to LEDs if they aren't already, and getting into the habit of unplugging chargers and appliances when they're not in use. Even small things like washing clothes at 30 degrees instead of 60 save a surprising amount of energy.

You're already thinking in the right direction — that's the most important step!

Lots of love,
Tobias

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'We are looking for reviews of documentaries about the environment or climate change. Write a review of a documentary you have watched, describing what it is about and saying whether you would recommend it.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Documentary Review: David Attenborough: A Life on Our Planet

This remarkable documentary, released in 2020, is both a witness statement and a vision for the future of our planet. David Attenborough, now in his nineties, reflects on the dramatic environmental changes he has witnessed throughout his long career as a naturalist and broadcaster.

The film is divided into two parts. The first is a genuinely moving account of environmental destruction — from the loss of rainforests to the collapse of fish populations — accompanied by beautiful and sometimes heartbreaking footage. The second half, however, offers a surprisingly hopeful roadmap for how humanity can restore the natural world.

Attenborough's calm, authoritative voice carries enormous weight, and the combination of personal reflection and scientific fact makes the film unusually compelling. Unlike some environmental documentaries, it avoids leaving the viewer feeling overwhelmed or guilty, instead inspiring action.

This is essential viewing for anyone who cares about the future of our planet. Thought-provoking, beautiful, and ultimately hopeful. Rating: 5/5
